input_candidates.truth_value: The 1.2C steel exhibits a superior combination of strength and ductility (UTS 946.7 MPa, elongation 56.2%), with UTS × TE of 53205 MPa·%.

input_candidates.truth_value: The C-J analysis reveals that the strain hardening behavior is linked to the evolution of dislocation structures: planar slip, Taylor lattice, microbands, and microband interactions.
